title: House
description: One house record (catalog product or building documentation). Enum values must come from data/ontology.json.

id.description: Globally unique numeric id. The on-disk key is `house-<id>`.

model.description: Human-readable name. For catalog records, the model/variant name; for documentation records, the building's address or descriptor.

source.description: Where the record came from. Enum: see ontology.sources.

source_origin.description: Free-form note on the source documents (e.g. 'scanned 1956 Baubeschreibung + Ansichten').

year_built.description: Year of original construction (existing buildings) or product introduction (catalog).

levels.description: Stack of inhabited levels, top-to-bottom in build order, e.g. ['KG','EG','OG','DG']. Enum values: see ontology.levels.

agent_notes.description: Hinweise für AI-Agenten, die das Haus modellieren.

images.description: Per-image metadata. Empty array if no images yet.

source_pdfs.description: List of source-PDF filenames in the house folder (auto-discovered if omitted).

bim_ai_blocking_issues.description: Tri-state: field missing OR null → not yet assessed (UI shows no badge). Empty list → assessed, no blockers (✓ modellierbar). Non-empty list → blocked by these issues until they close. Format: 'owner/repo#NNN'. Cache in data/.issue_state.json, refresh via `make refresh-issue-state`.

file.description: Filename relative to the house folder (e.g. 'hanse_variant35172_exterior10.original.avif').

view.description: Enum: see ontology.image_views. Only meaningful for exteriors/elevations.

floor.description: Enum: see ontology.levels. Only meaningful for floorplans.
